arts.ttl: Barcelona 1-2 Atletico Madrid: Player Ratings From Atlético's Shocking Stoppage Time Victory
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid defeated Barcelona in a thrilling match and will end the year atop the standings in La Liga.

arts.ttl: Alexander Sørloth stuns Barcelona with last-gasp winner for Atlético Madrid
arts.athr: Sid Lowe at the Estadio Olímpic Lluís Companys
arts.sum: They were 95 minutes into the battle that been built as the clash that would decide the title, the final seconds slipping away, when a man who looks for all the world like a norse warrior delivered the decisive blow. Atlético Madrid had suffered, they had resisted and, in truth, they had probably longed for that final whistle to go, and then suddenly Alex Sørloth thumped in another late winner. In doing so he sent Atlético Madrid top the table at Christmas and their coach, staff and subs sprinting onto the pitch. Barcelona’s fans meanwhile turned and headed straight for the exit, unable to believe what had just happened. What had happened was this: on a night when they had more than enough chances to win it, including deep into added time, in which Jan Oblak, the goalkeeper had been the best player on the pitch, with Pedri, they had somehow been beaten. It is the first time they have ever fallen at the hands of a Diego Simeone led Atlético team and it hurt. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: THREE clubs in for Rashford, Barca’s ‘best deal ever’, Nkunku future: FootballTransfers Recap
arts.sum: The January transfer window continues to approach at a pace, with positive movement for Manchester United, Barcelona and Chelsea among others. With Rashford not expected to feature for Manchester United on Sunday against Bournemouth, speculation continues to mount as to the where the England international may wind up in the January transfer window.
